title: EU Import Control System 2 - Release 3 goes live on 3 June 2024.
bodyHtml:
The letter received from the Turkish Exporters Assembly states that, in accordance with the Union Customs Code (UCC) in effect in the EU as of May 1, 2016, efforts have been made for the electronic execution of all customs procedures, including data transmission and storage between customs authorities and economic operators. Information technology systems developed for this purpose are gradually being implemented.

One of these electronic systems, the Import Control System 2 (ICS2), requires economic operators responsible for shipments arriving in or transiting through the EU to submit entry summary declaration data to EU customs via ICS2 before arrival. The system, planned to be implemented in three versions, had its first version launched on March 15, 2021, for "air cargo pre-loading" and "airmail pre-loading" processes. The second version came into effect on March 1, 2023, for "air cargo transportation," "air transportation," and "postal transportation."

Now, the third version is scheduled to be gradually implemented in maritime, railway, and road transport on June 3, 2024. Maritime transport companies are required to start the process by December 4, 2024, and additionally, the filing of house-level documents for these companies is set to begin by December 4, 2024, and no later than April 1, 2025. Railway and road transport companies are also expected to start implementing this version by September 1, 2025, with the version coming into effect on April 1, 2025.

In this context, a technical guide and brochure for Release 3 , which will be activated by the European Commission as of March 1, 2024, have been prepared. It is planned to conduct a webinar for Release 3  to ensure the compliance of Turkish maritime and road transport with the process and to safeguard against potential uncertainties. Further information will be provided after the confirmation of the webinar date.
